steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the text. The passage shows Daniel Pipestone collecting petition signatures: he keeps the petition with him always, spots unsigned members everywhere, and corners them in any circumstance. Every detail illustrates one thing: the intensity of Daniel's effort to gather signatures.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To show that attitudes toward the petition within the tribal community change over time: The passage never describes a change in attitudes over time — it is a static portrait of Daniel's behavior. ✗
  • Choice B — To demonstrate that most tribal members are enthusiastic about signing the petition: The passage does not describe members' enthusiasm; it describes Daniel pursuing them in every situation. Nothing suggests eager volunteers. ✗
  • Choice C — To portray Daniel Pipestone's strong commitment to collecting signatures for the petition: Every sentence supports this — keeping the petition with him always, sharpening his eyes for unsigned members, cornering them anywhere regardless of circumstance. ✓
  • Choice D — To suggest that some tribal members refuse to sign the petition because they dislike Daniel Pipestone: The passage mentions no refusals and no personal animosity toward Daniel. ✗
That matches Choice C — To portray Daniel Pipestone's strong commitment to collecting signatures for the petition.
